channel.title: 2026-03-16 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-03-16 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: [ASAP] Quantum Chemistry-Driven Molecular Inverse Design of Stable Isomers with Data-Free Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Francesco Calcagno, Luca Serfilippi, Giorgio Franceschelli, Marco Garavelli, Mirco Musolesi, and Ivan Rivalta
Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation DOI: 10.1021/acs.jctc.5c02055

item.title: Data-efficient quantum noise modeling via machine learning
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Yanjun Ji, Marco Roth, David A. Kreplin, Ilia Polian, and Frank K. Wilhelm
Author(s): Yanjun Ji, Marco Roth, David A. Kreplin, Ilia Polian, and Frank K. Wilhelm Noise-aware compilation on near-term quantum processors requires accurate noise models, but standard ones often miss algorithm- and hardware-specific error mechanisms, and full characterization can be costly. In this study a data-efficient framework combines a physically motivated parametrized noise model with Bayesian optimization, to infer algorithm- and hardware-specific error parameters from routine circuit-execution data. Remarkably, models trained only on small-circuit data generalize well to larger validation circuits, yielding 65% better model fidelity. This provides a scalable, low-overhead route to more predictive, application-aware noise models for quantum compilation workflows. [Phys. Rev. Ap…

item.title: MPS-VMC: A high-performance matrix product state variational Monte Carlo Solver for ab initio quantum chemistry
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
Publication date: May 2026 Source: Computer Physics Communications, Volume 322 Author(s): Zhiqian Xu, Yangjun Wu, Lizhong Fu, Chu Guo, Honghui Shang

item.title: Toward ab initio quantum simulations of atomic nuclei using noisy qubits
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect Publication: Science Bulletin
Publication date: Available online 4 March 2026 Source: Science Bulletin Author(s): Chongji Jiang, Junchen Pei, Rongzhe Hu, Shaoliang Jin, Haoyu Shang, Siqin Fan, Furong Xu

item.title: [ASAP] Orbital Topology Overcomes Energetic Constraints: Conjugation Expansion Breaks the Inverse Relationship between SOMO Splitting and Spin Coupling for Giant Ferromagnetic Coupling in Organic Diradicals
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Xiangyun Zheng, Zhiru Zhang, Xinyu Song, and Yuxiang Bu
The Journal of Physical Chemistry Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.jpclett.6c00565

item.title: Finite-temperature ferroelectric phase transitions from machine-learned force fields
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Kristoffer Eggestad, Ida C. Skogvoll, Øystein Gullbrekken, Benjamin A. D. Williamson, and Sverre M. Selbach
Author(s): Kristoffer Eggestad, Ida C. Skogvoll, Øystein Gullbrekken, Benjamin A. D. Williamson, and Sverre M. Selbach Machine-learned force fields (MLFFs) can bring first-principles accuracy to finite-temperature molecular dynamics for materials simulations. Here, the authors use MLFFs, trained on-the-fly using only ground-state structures, to predict phase transitions in the prototypical ferroelectrics BaTiO 3 , PbTiO 3 , LiNbO 3 , and BiFeO 3 . Order parameter discontinuities, mixed order–disorder and displacive character, and space groups are correctly predicted, while exact transition temperatures are functional-dependent. This demonstrates both the promise and current limitations of MLFFs for simulating the thermal evolution of materials, where long-range electrostatic interactions…

item.title: Modeling magnetoelastic wave interactions in magnetic films and heterostructures: A finite-difference approach
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Peter Flauger, Matthias Küß, Michael K. Steinbauer, Florian Bruckner, Bernhard Emhofer, Emeline Nysten, Matthias Weiß, Dieter Suess, Hubert J. Krenner, Manfred Albrecht, and Claas Abert
Author(s): Peter Flauger, Matthias Küß, Michael K. Steinbauer, Florian Bruckner, Bernhard Emhofer, Emeline Nysten, Matthias Weiß, Dieter Suess, Hubert J. Krenner, Manfred Albrecht, and Claas Abert The (inverse) magnetostrictive effect in ferromagnets couples the magnetic properties to the mechanical stress, allowing for an interaction between the magnetic and mechanical degrees of freedom. In this work, we present a time-integration scheme for the self-consistent simulation of coupled magneto… [Phys. Rev. Applied 25, 034050] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Persistent Spin Currents in Superconducting Altermagnets
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. X
👤 Kyle Monkman, Joan Weng, Niclas Heinsdorf, Alberto Nocera, Yafis Barlas, and Marcel Franz
Author(s): Kyle Monkman, Joan Weng, Niclas Heinsdorf, Alberto Nocera, Yafis Barlas, and Marcel Franz Persistent spin currents in superconducting altermagnets offer a dissipationless mechanism for spin transport that could enable the development of high-efficiency spintronic computer chips. [Phys. Rev. X 16, 011057] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Spin Supercurrents in Superconducting Altermagnets
item.description:
📚 Physics
👤 Huaiming Guo
Author(s): Huaiming Guo Materials from a new class of magnets could host permanent dissipationless spin currents when they enter a superconducting state. [Physics 19, 33] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
